tasks.title: Add single-alert triage eval scenario
tasks.description: Create evals/single_alert_triage.spec.ts in kbn-evals-suite-security-alert-triage. Exercises ALERT_ATTACHMENT_PROMPT via callConverse with a single alert attachment and asserts that the alert-analysis skill is invoked and the response contains a markdown-formatted disposition recommendation.
acceptanceCriteria: File x-pack/solutions/security/packages/kbn-evals-suite-security-alert-triage/evals/single_alert_triage.spec.ts exists
TypeScript type check passes for the suite: node scripts/type_check --project x-pack/solutions/security/packages/kbn-evals-suite-security-alert-triage/tsconfig.json
ESLint passes: node scripts/eslint x-pack/solutions/security/packages/kbn-evals-suite-security-alert-triage/evals/single_alert_triage.spec.ts
The spec imports and uses callConverse from ../src/converse_task
The spec uses createSkillInvocationEvaluator with skillName: 'alert-analysis'
The evaluators array includes evaluators.criteria with at least 5 named criteria
The evaluators array includes Object.values(evaluators.traceBasedEvaluators)
metadata.expectedAttachmentReads is set to 0 on the example
The metadata.attachments array contains exactly one entry with type 'security.alerts'
tasks.notes: Follow alert_triage_quality.spec.ts exactly — same fixture extension pattern, same dataset shape, same evaluator composition. Use generateSyntheticAlert (or equivalent) from src/synthetic_alerts.ts for the fixture alert. A single alert does NOT trigger summary mode so expectedAttachmentReads must be 0 — the existing attachmentReadCompliance evaluator will pass trivially, but include it for consistency. The beforeAll hook must insert the alert into .alerts-security.alerts-default via esClient. The afterAll hook must delete it.

tasks.title: Add EASE alert summary eval spec and task function
tasks.description: Create src/ease_summary_task.ts (task function mirroring use_alert_summary.tsx's HTTP call) and evals/ease_alert_summary.spec.ts (CODE evaluator verifying the response is parseable JSON with non-empty summary and recommendedActions keys). Directly addresses Jon's concern about silent JSON.parse failures in the EASE flyout.
acceptanceCriteria: File x-pack/solutions/security/packages/kbn-evals-suite-security-alert-triage/src/ease_summary_task.ts exists
File x-pack/solutions/security/packages/kbn-evals-suite-security-alert-triage/evals/ease_alert_summary.spec.ts exists
TypeScript type check passes for the suite: node scripts/type_check --project x-pack/solutions/security/packages/kbn-evals-suite-security-alert-triage/tsconfig.json

ESLint passes: node scripts/eslint x-pack/solutions/security/packages/kbn-evals-suite-security-alert-triage/evals/ease_alert_summary.spec.ts x-pack/solutions/security/packages/kbn-evals-suite-security-alert-triage/src/ease_summary_task.ts
The EASE evaluator is kind: 'CODE' — there is no LLM evaluator in this spec
The evaluator attempts JSON.parse on the raw response and returns score 0 with an explanation if it throws
The evaluator checks that parsed.summary is a non-empty string
The evaluator checks that parsed.recommendedActions is a non-empty string
The spec does NOT include evaluators.traceBasedEvaluators
ease_summary_task.ts posts to the actions/connector execute route with promptIds: { promptGroupId: 'ease', promptId: 'alertSummarySystemPrompt' }
tasks.notes: Read use_alert_summary.tsx (public/flyout/ease/hooks/) and post_actions_connector_execute.ts (elastic_assistant/server/routes/) before writing the task function — the request body shape must match exactly what the EASE hook sends. The CODE evaluator pattern to follow is in src/evaluators.ts (attachmentReadCompliance). Do NOT include traceBasedEvaluators — the actions/connector execute route does not emit a trace_id in the same shape as Agent Builder. The alert context for the dataset example should be a minimal plain-text string (rule name, severity, host, one-line reason) — no Elasticsearch insertion needed.

tasks.title: Sync prompt changes to elastic/integrations
tasks.description: Clone elastic/integrations, run yarn generate-security-ai-prompts, copy the generated prompt JSON files to the integrations package, bump the package version, add a changelog entry, and open a PR against elastic/integrations main.
acceptanceCriteria: elastic/integrations is cloned at ~/Repos/integrations
yarn generate-security-ai-prompts exits with code 0
All .json files from target/security_ai_prompts/ have been copied to ~/Repos/integrations/packages/security_ai_prompts/kibana/security_ai_prompt/
The version in ~/Repos/integrations/packages/security_ai_prompts/manifest.yml has been incremented
A new entry has been prepended to ~/Repos/integrations/packages/security_ai_prompts/changelog.yml referencing elastic/kibana#271330
A PR has been opened against elastic/integrations main and its URL has been logged to stdout
tasks.notes: Read x-pack/solutions/security/packages/security-ai-prompts/README.md before starting — it contains the authoritative steps and exact paths. If yarn generate-security-ai-prompts is not in package.json scripts, check for node scripts/generate_security_ai_prompts.js. Increment patch version only (1.2.3 → 1.2.4). Changelog entry format must match the existing entries in changelog.yml. Branch name: sync/security-ai-prompts-kibana-271330.
